content: Phase transition success rates (approximate, varies by study and era): Phase I to Phase II: 60-70% (safety/PK issues cause most failures); Phase II to Phase III: 30-40% (efficacy signal insufficient or safety concerns emerge); Phase III to approval: 50-60% (failure to confirm efficacy, safety signals in larger populations, or commercial considerations). Oncology has particularly low Phase II-III success (~25-30%) due to reliance on surrogate endpoints that don't always predict survival benefit. CNS/psychiatry also has high failure rates (~80-90% in Phase II/III) due to poor translational models and high placebo responses. Strategies to improve success include: better target validation, biomarker-driven patient selection, adaptive designs, earlier go/no-go decisions, and learning from failed programs.
